summary: Finished matching and linking the fire item translation unit by adding the missing itfire ItemStateTable data, correcting two sdata2 symbols from global it_* names to local @ labels, and switching melee/it/items/itfire.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev report credited this PR with one new match for main/melee/it/items/itfire .data, moving that data from 0.00% to 100.00%, plus +976 linked code bytes, +48 matched data bytes, and +56 linked data bytes.
what_changed: The new state table contains three entries for motion IDs 0, 1, and 2, each wiring the corresponding itFire_UnkMotionN_Anim, itFire_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itFire_UnkMotionN_Coll callbacks.
Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itfire.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itfire.c").
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so sdata2 addresses 0x804DD3A0 and 0x804DD3A4 are local float symbols @145 and @160 instead of global symbols it_804DD3A0 and it_804DD3A4.
Bot report showed one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itfire, from 0.00% before to 100.00% after.
smart_moves: Completed the translation unit by restoring the concrete item state table rather than only matching code bodies; the bot specifically attributes the new match to .data.
Kept the item state table compact and idiomatic for this codebase: ItemStateTable array entries map numeric state IDs directly to Anim/Phys/Coll callback triples.
Adjusted symbol scope/naming for the relevant sdata2 floats to local @ labels, avoiding unnecessary global it_* names where the matched output expected local constants.
Only marked itfire.c as Matching after the data and symbol map changes were in place, tying build configuration to demonstrated match status.
decomp_lessons: For item files, a unit may remain nonmatching until its associated .data tables are reconstructed, even if nearby functions are already present.
Item state tables should be checked when finishing an item file: missing ItemStateTable data can be the final piece needed for a .data match.
Symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt are part of the matching surface. Converting incorrectly global sdata2 constants to local @ labels can be necessary for accurate linkage and symbol matching.
After a translation unit reaches match status, update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching so linked-code progress is reflected by the build.
The evidence in this PR is concise and mostly mechanical; there was no PR body or human review discussion explaining how the table was derived.
naming_conventions: Item state table symbol used address-style naming: it_803F7C10.
Fire item callbacks use the pattern itFire_UnkMotion<N>_<Anim|Phys|Coll>.
Item files in configure.py are referenced as paths like melee/it/items/itfire.c under Object(Matching, ...).
Local sdata2 constants in symbols.txt use @number labels, while address-style it_804Dxxxx names imply global symbols; this PR changed two floats to local @ labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reconstruct missing .data by declaring an ItemStateTable array with exact callback ordering for each motion state.
Match symbol visibility/scope in the symbols map to the original output: two sdata2 float constants were made local labels at 0x804DD3A0 and 0x804DD3A4.
Use the decomp.dev progress report to validate match impact; here it confirmed +48 bytes matched data and 100% .data for the itfire unit.
Promote the object from NonMatching to Matching only after the data table and symbol corrections allow the unit to link cleanly.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only feedback-like record was the decomp.dev bot report showing linked code, matched data, linked data, and one new .data match for itfire.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs where adding an ItemStateTable completed a .data match.
Search for remaining NonMatching melee/it/items files in configure.py that may only need state table data.
Compare itfire ItemStateTable layout with itlizardon, itfreezer, itthunder, and other Pokémon item files.
Audit sdata2 symbols around it_804DD3A0 through it_804DD3AC for local-vs-global label consistency.
